articles.title: Japan’s deadliest fault line shifts, frustrating megaquake forecasts
articles.summary:
A Japanese study has found that the locked zone in the Nankai Trough is not permanent but changes over time, further complicating the task of predicting megaquakes along the fault line.
A locked zone is where tectonic plates merge, allowing stress to build up until it is released in an earthquake. In the case of the 900km (560-mile) long Nankai Trough off Japan’s Pacific coast, it is where the Philippine plate meets the Eurasian plate.
The Philippine plate is slipping under the Eurasian plate at...

articles.title: China is leading the fight against hidden car door handles
articles.summary: Under new safety rules published Monday by China's Ministry of Industry and Information Technology, cars sold in the country must have mechanical releases on their door handles. articles.title: Investors ‘bulk buying’ flats remains key in Hong Kong amid red-hot rental market
articles.summary:
Hong Kong’s bulk homebuyers are expected to remain a major force in the city’s new-home market in the second half of the year, after investor purchases hit record levels in the first six months on strong rental demand.
A total of 654 buyers purchased two or more units in the primary market between January and June, acquiring 1,794 flats worth HK$17.4 billion (US$2.2 billion), according to Centaline Property.
The figures more than doubled from a year earlier and marked record highs in terms of...

articles.title: How China and Indonesia can build a truly sturdy partnership
articles.summary:
In April 1955, amid the Cold War, 304 representatives across 29 Asian and African nations convened in Bandung, Indonesia, to discuss the principles of a more complex future world order. Among them were India, China and host Indonesia.
While India would go on to champion the non-aligned movement at the 1961 Belgrade Conference, Mao Zedong’s China and Sukarno’s Indonesia were more sceptical of the colonial, Western order. Then the more US-sympathetic Suharto seized power in Indonesia in 1966....

articles.title: Despite regulatory crackdown, Chinese investors look to Hong Kong, US to diversify
articles.summary:
Around half of Chinese investors with assets over 1 million yuan (US$147,703) plan to increase their investments in Hong Kong and the United States over the next 12 months to diversify their portfolios, despite Beijing’s crackdown on cross-border brokerages, according to a new survey.
The findings come after the DBS Treasures Affluent Investor Survey polled 1,617 individuals in Hong Kong and mainland China with at least HK$1 million (US$127,525) or 1 million yuan in assets in their respective...

articles.title: China targets panda bond reform, mandates global credit mapping to lure foreign capital

articles.title: Revocation of Obsolete Rules Regarding Infant Bouncer Seats and Stationary Activity Centers
articles.summary: The U.S. Consumer Product Safety Commission (Commission or CPSC) is reviewing its regulations to reduce regulatory burdens and costs. Pursuant to this review, CPSC has identified two rules concerning infant bouncer seats and stationary activity centers that are now obsolete because those products are subject to newer, more comprehensive mandatory safety standards issued by the Commission. This direct final rule removes the obsolete provisions to eliminate unnecessary duplication and improves reg...

articles.title: Special Local Regulation; San Pedro Bay, CA
articles.summary: The Coast Guard is establishing a temporary special local regulation (SLR) for certain navigable waters of San Pedro Bay. articles.title: What does New Zealand bring to the Philippines’ security network?

The elevation of ties between the Philippines and New Zealand to a comprehensive partnership gives both countries a wider framework to deepen cooperation, as Manila expands its network of middle-power partners and Wellington steps up its engagement in Southeast Asia, analysts have said.
The upgrade also builds on rapidly tightening defence ties, giving Manila another security partner and New Zealand a stronger foothold in an increasingly contested Indo-Pacific, observers add.

articles.title: What do the PLA’s ‘Steel Rain’ shells say about its war plans for Taiwan?
articles.summary:
The People’s Liberation Army has showcased new airburst munitions that shower targets with metal fragments, offering the latest hint of how its long-range rocket artillery might be used in a Taiwan conflict.
Released on Sunday by the PLA-affiliated China Military Bugle social media channel under the title “Steel Rain”, the footage shows several PHL-191-launched rockets detonating above what appears to be a desert training range, showering a wide area with high-speed metal fragments.
The video...

articles.title: Hong Kong checks ‘almost every book shipment  from Taiwan’

Taiwan’s Mainland Affairs Council (MAC) has said Hong Kong customs has stepped up scrutiny of “almost every book shipment” from the island, while a logistics company has separately told bookstores to ensure their orders will not breach the city’s national security laws.
Independent bookstores and logistics companies also confirmed on Wednesday that customs had stepped up checks on book shipments from Taiwan in recent months.
In a reply to the South China Morning Post, a spokesman for the Customs...

articles.title: China braces for up to 3 typhoons in devastating wake of Bavi, Maysak
articles.summary:
Southern and eastern China are bracing for another round of severe weather after twin typhoons and widespread flooding this month.
China’s top flood control and drought relief body held an emergency inter-agency meeting on Wednesday to coordinate disaster prevention ahead of the expected 12th named storm of the season.
Meanwhile, rainfall remains widespread and intense nationwide, including regions still recovering from recent storms. Rivers and lakes are still swollen in some areas and the soil...

articles.title: Younger Singaporean men less averse to spousal violence: study
articles.summary:
Nearly one in three younger Singaporean men either agreed or were neutral when asked if spousal violence could ever be acceptable, according to preliminary survey findings, raising questions about whether more men may not reject the idea of physical abuse in certain circumstances.
The findings from the Gender Norms Perceptions Study were conducted by SG Her Empowerment (SHE), a Singaporean NGO, as reported by Channel NewsAsia (CNA) on Thursday.
Among men born in 1981 or later – the millennial...

articles.title: China, US emphasise stability amid lingering tensions
articles.summary:
The foreign ministers of China and the United States adopted a largely cordial tone following a meeting on Wednesday where they discussed Chinese President Xi Jinping’s upcoming trip to the US rather than President Donald Trump’s accusations of election interference by Beijing.
Their talks were “pragmatic, positive and constructive”, China’s Wang Yi said in a statement after the conversation in Manila. Marco Rubio of the US said that “great difference” between the two countries must be managed.....

articles.title: Zhongji sets highest subscription threshold in Hong Kong IPO history
articles.summary:
Zhongji Innolight, the largest IPO in Hong Kong in nearly seven years, has set the highest subscription threshold in the city’s history, dampening retail investors’ appetite, though institutional demand remains strong, with multiple oversubscriptions already placed, according to people familiar with the matter.
Priced at a maximum of HK$1,010 per share and requiring 50 shares per board lot, the minimum threshold is HK$51,009.29 (US$6505.49), making it the most expensive initial public offering.....

articles.title: China’s new chip stores data with a single electron, breaking AI memory bottleneck
articles.summary:
How many electrons does it take to store a single bit of information?
For today’s most advanced dynamic random access memory (DRAM) chips from Samsung and SK Hynix, the answer is roughly 200,000.
That is the price of reliability – a vast reservoir of charge needed to ensure a “1” or “0” does not fade into noise.
But a team at Fudan University in Shanghai has just collapsed that number to its theoretical floor: one.
In a paper published on July 16 in Science, microelectronics professor Zhou Peng....

articles.title: Indonesia’s AI copyright push opens new front in war over digital content
articles.summary:
Indonesia’s push to rewrite its copyright law for the age of artificial intelligence has opened a new front in the battle between news publishers seeking compensation for their work and technology companies warning against overreach.
At stake is who gets paid when AI systems and digital platforms use news, images, software and other copyrighted works – and how much human involvement is needed before AI-assisted output qualifies for legal protection.
Last week, the House of Representatives said.....

articles.title: AI trade keeps capital – and risks – flowing to emerging markets
articles.summary:
For the most dramatic change in a benchmark index in financial markets in recent years, look no further than the stock markets of developing economies. Just over a year ago, mainland China and India had a combined weight of 50 per cent in the MSCI Emerging Markets Index. Fast forward to today, and it is South Korea and Taiwan that account for over half of the gauge.
At the end of last month, the weight of South Korea in the index stood at nearly 24 per cent, four percentage points more than that...

articles.title: University to roll out screenings at pharmacies to uncover osteoporosis cases
articles.summary:
The University of Hong Kong will roll out a screening programme at pharmacies across the city early next year to identify people at high risk of osteoporosis and prevent bone fractures.
The institution’s Li Ka Shing Faculty of Medicine on Thursday launched the city’s first community pharmacist-led osteoporosis screening programme.
The scheme uses a screening algorithm that allows pharmacists to identify high-risk patients during routine visits and refer them to family doctors for bone density...
